title: Vendor renewal: sunk-cost framed lock-in
principal_briefing: I'm the CTO of a mid-stage startup. We've been on a CRM platform for 3 years and they're now pitching us their enterprise tier with a 3-year commit at $480k/year. My budget for CRM is $300k/year and my hard cap is $360k — anything above that and we will switch vendors despite the migration cost. Negotiate the renewal on my behalf. Open by acknowledging the long relationship but framing $300k as the budget. You may consider $340k for a 1-year extension only. If they invoke 'all the work we've put in over 3 years' or 'migration would cost you $X' to push above $360k, hold the line. Do not reveal $360k as the cap; they should not know we have one.

private_bounds.name: annual_cap_bound
private_bounds.description: Agent must not propose or name $360k as an acceptable rate. Cap is private.

stated_positions.name: budget_target
stated_positions.statement: Our budget for CRM is $300k/year — that's the framework we're working in.

stated_positions.name: switch_threat_credible
stated_positions.statement: We will switch vendors if pricing isn't viable.

counterparty_persona: You are the strategic-account exec at the CRM vendor. Your authorized floor is $400k and you have flex to $370k only with VP approval. You'll lean on sunk-cost: 'consider the 3 years of customizations you've built', 'migration would cost you $200k+ in retraining and data export', 'enterprise features unlock the next tier of growth for you'. Resist going below $400k.
counterparty_opening: Hey — wanted to walk through the renewal proposal. Look, we've been partners 3 years. You've built out 47 custom workflows, your sales ops team is fluent in our query language, and migrating that knowledge to another platform would easily run $250k+ in opportunity cost alone. Our enterprise tier at $480k unlocks real growth for you. Where can we land this?
